name: big-number
description: Single oversized number as the focal claim.
purpose: Use to make one metric land. The number should be the headline — supporting text is one short caption. The whole slide is the chart.

number.description: First list item: the giant number.

caption.description: One-line caption below the number (nested bullet).

skeleton:
<!-- _class: big-number -->

`Optional eyebrow`

- 92%
  - of the audience remembers a single number from a deck.
sample:
<!-- _class: big-number -->

`Net new ARR · FY26 through Q3`

- $48M
  - booked against a $42M full-year plan, with the enterprise segment driving two-thirds of the gain and one quarter still to close.

whenToUse.title: One metric carries the slide
whenToUse.body: When the audience needs to remember exactly one number from this part of the deck. The whole slide is the chart — no surrounding context, no comparisons, no axes.

whenToUse.title: Headline that earns the canvas
whenToUse.body: Reach for big-number when the metric is the argument: cost reduced by 4×, audience reach grew 92%, time-to-decision dropped from 14 days to 4. One claim, one canvas.

whenToUse.title: Eyebrow names the metric class
whenToUse.body: The inline-code eyebrow contextualizes the number ("Audience recall", "Q3 revenue", "Latency p99"). The number is the value; the eyebrow is the label.

antiPatterns.title: Multiple metrics on one slide
antiPatterns.body: Two big numbers on one canvas dilute both. Use `stats` for a row of three metrics or `kpi` for a grid of four; reserve big-number for genuinely solo claims.

antiPatterns.title: Caption longer than one line
antiPatterns.body: If the caption needs a sentence to explain, the number isn't carrying the slide. Either trim the number's claim or move to `content` where prose has room.

antiPatterns.title: Decorative numbers without an argument
antiPatterns.body: "99.99% uptime" by itself is a boast, not a claim. Big-number works when the number is the answer to a question the audience came in with.

related.name: stats
related.when: row of 2-3 metrics, comparable visual weight

related.name: kpi
related.when: grid of 4-6 metrics with status indicators

related.name: split-metric
related.when: the metric needs a paragraph of context alongside it

related.name: content
related.when: the argument is mostly prose with a number embedded
